movie.title: 23 000 Lives
movie.overview: Unable to stand by and watch as countless refugees lose their lives attempting to reach Europe by sea, a group of young Berliners take action. Inexperienced but driven by passion and courage, they launch a crowdfunding campaign to buy an old ship. Venturing into the Mediterranean, they embark on a mission to save thousands of people. But their efforts soon put their understanding of law and justice to the test.

aiEnrichment.summary_one_sentence: A group of young Berliners crowdfund a ship to rescue refugees in the Mediterranean, challenging their own beliefs about law and justice.
aiEnrichment.summary_short: A group of young Berliners, unwilling to ignore the deaths of refugees crossing the sea to Europe, launch a crowdfunding campaign to buy an old ship. Inexperienced but passionate, they sail into the Mediterranean to save lives. Their mission quickly confronts them with legal and moral dilemmas. The experience tests their understanding of justice and the limits of civilian action.
aiEnrichment.summary_long: In Berlin, a group of young activists are devastated by news of thousands of refugees dying at sea while trying to reach Europe. Unable to stand by, they organize a crowdfunding campaign to purchase an old ship for rescue missions. Despite having no maritime or humanitarian experience, their courage and passion drive them forward. They outfit the vessel and set sail into the dangerous waters of the Mediterranean. Upon encountering overloaded boats and desperate passengers, they begin rescuing people directly at sea. However, they soon face resistance from authorities and ambiguous international laws regarding civilian rescues. Their actions draw scrutiny and legal threats, forcing them to question what is lawful versus what is morally right. Internal conflicts emerge as the group grapples with the consequences of their mission. Over time, their understanding of justice, responsibility, and activism is profoundly transformed. The journey becomes as much about self-discovery as it is about saving lives.
aiEnrichment.synopsis: 23 000 Lives follows a group of idealistic young Berliners who are moved to action by the ongoing refugee crisis in the Mediterranean. Pooling resources through crowdfunding, they acquire an old ship and venture into international waters to conduct search-and-rescue operations. The documentary-style drama explores their transition from inexperienced volunteers to frontline responders. As they confront the harsh realities of migration, border politics, and maritime law, the group must navigate impossible choices. The film examines themes of empathy, civil disobedience, and the personal cost of standing up for human lives.

story_structure.climax: Their mission brings them into direct confrontation with questions of law and justice, testing their convictions and the limits of their actions.
story_structure.conflict: They launch a crowdfunding campaign to buy an old ship and face the challenge of undertaking dangerous Mediterranean rescues as untrained volunteers.
story_structure.resolution: The group is left to reconcile their humanitarian goals with the legal realities they faced, with the outcome of their understanding and efforts revealed by the story's end.
story_structure.introduction: A group of young Berliners, unable to ignore the deaths of refugees at sea, are moved to act despite having no prior experience in rescue operations.
story_structure.risingAction: After acquiring the vessel, they venture into the Mediterranean and begin saving people, but encounter growing tension with legal and political authorities.
